{"user_message": "why are exponents preformed before multiplication in the order of operations", "detected_tone": "informal", "formality_level": 3, "tone_markers": ["question", "curious"], "matching_response": "Hey! Good question. The order of operations (PEMDAS/BODMAS) is just a convention that helps keep calculations consistent. Exponents come before multiplication because they’re considered a higher priority operation—think of them as “raising to a power” which is a more fundamental operation than just multiplying numbers together. So when you see something like 2 × 3², you first calculate 3² (which is 9) and then multiply by 2, giving 18. This rule keeps everyone on the same page when solving equations."}
